pages.text:
Personal Training Client Application Form

Full legal name: Shane

Country: US

Age: 35

Height: 6ft 1in

Weight: 181 lb

Body fat percentage (plus measurement technique): 19.8% with friend's Skulpt

Years of training experience: 6 months 5x5

For the section below, please give your current maximum abilities in weight
times reps, e.g. 250 lb x 6.

Bench press: 140lb x 5

Squat (excluding body weight): 230lb x 5

Chin-up (excluding body weight): 3 bodyweight chin ups

Overhead press: 90lb x 5

What is your occupation?
I do finance in an office setting. Normally wake up at 7am and sleep around
midnight or just before.

PEDs: No

Activity level: Sedentary (e.g. office job)

Gym Access: Your standard globo gym. No reverse hyper
headings: Body fat percentage (plus measurement technique): 19.8% with friend’s Skulpt
For the section below, please give your current maximum abilities in weight
Gym Access: Your standard globo gym. No reverse hyper

pages.text:
Please list all the supplements you are currently taking: Whey, creatine, fish oil ,
mutlivitamin

Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail:

I have been doing keto for about a year. I have at times tracked my macros and
cooked my food and would be willing to do that for a lot of my meals and have the
other meals be keto from a service that tells me the food macros. Right now I eat
whenever I'm hungry but think I could do a more set schedule. 3 meals and
something before bed would work. I am used to eating something like that
schedule. I don't like eating under 1700 calories a day. Currently my average
calorie intake to maintain with 3 days a week of lifting is 2500.

I don't really go off keto or cheat. If I do it's with high fat food. I don't like having
to readjust to keto after a carb feed.

I have lost about 30 lbs in the last year on keto. I would really like to get to a six
pack level which i think will be around 12 to 14% bf and get more muscular.
Getting lean and muscle growth is more important right now than how I do in
bench , squat , and deadlift.

My weightloss has slowed down recently and now I want something more
structured that I know will get me results. When I was losing 1lb a week that was
great and that is something I would like to be able to do again.

Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train? If so,
please list them:
Really would prefer to train after work. I finish between 5 or 6 most days. I can eat
before and/or after but don't want to go home first or I won't hit gym.

Fitness Summary

I have trained for 3 months here and there the last 5 years. However I have been
consistent with 3 days a week of 5x5 for the last 6 months and would like to do that
or 4 days a week on a S,M,W,F schedule after work.
headings: Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail:
Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train? If so,

pages.text:
James' Analysis

This is more of late 20's to mid 30's bro I have been targeting and speaking with
more. Many tend to have tried some IIFYM stuff before and now are doing Keto and
lost weight with it. They also usually have had some success with something like 5
x 5 but now feel like they are slowing down in weightloss and gym growth.

I wouldn't have gotten all this info just from sending out a sheet like this. I usually
do a phone call with people and would have gotten this summary.

I am going to give him the 24 hour anabolic window and I would want to get him
doing a full strength lifting routing 4 days a week on SMWF. So this will just have
Training and Rest days for calories.

Here are how I would calculate his macros :

Notes on that. In this case he will cook or order something that is whole food
related so I am giving him the 1.15 for Thermal Effect, assuming it will be better
than just cream cheese but not lean beef and veggies.

I think my program for him would be over 60 minutes but I still prefer a more
conservative calorie estimate.

I don't really use the energy balance factor than to get a general rule of thumb
what percent of a deficit is will be their floor days.

So here is how I would break up calories over the week

Maintenance Calories
For 4 Days a Week
Training
My
Recommendation

This is how I would set the daily meal macros. Protein is just a bit above 1.8g/kg
because 150 is an easy number to remember and work with. 25 net is my usually
starting point for carbs in keto and if they can do a few more without hunger issues
then that is fine. The rest is fat. I use 0.3 g/kg protein in each meal as a minimum.
I think some of the meals are a bit small and would let them usually adjust that to
their liking.

Nutrients are concentrated in the 6 hour window around training with the
expectation in this case that they would eat an hour or two before training as they

indicated they could and then and hour or two after training. Then the following
rest day there is a decent size breakfast. This breakdown was an attempt to do
something like Kevin's Rest day 1.

Personal Training Client Application Form

Full legal name: Shane

Country: US

midnight or just before.

PEDs: No

I don't really go off keto or cheat. If I do it's with high fat food. I don't like having

I have lost about 30 lbs in the last year on keto. I would really like to get to a six

Getting lean and muscle growth is more important right now than how I do in

My weightloss has slowed down recently and now I want something more
